what_it_does: Provides step-by-step build instructions for eight functional groups of a channel-memory/recall system, including dimension-aware chunking, served recall, lenses, retrieve-then-synthesize panel, channel context, multi-project/multi-session handling, projection, and spin-up/fork-fleet, with file targets and do/don't guardrails.
observations: Organized into eight groups (1-8), each with a principle, sequence, do/don't list, and target files.
Group 1 proposes structure-aware chunking that splits on the message's own paragraphs/sentences rather than a fixed character window, preserving parent attribution.
Group 2 specifies a bridge-free, Company-served recall substrate using embed :8007 and rerank :8008, plus a freshness check against source .jsonl mtime/size.
Group 3 introduces a requirements/preferences lens that is explicitly anti-recency and reads a cross-session feedback-* corpus before layering session-specific Tim signals.
Group 3 also includes a spin-up-choosing lens that scores fork-point candidates by decision density, expertise crystallization, open threads, and novelty.
Group 4 designs a retrieve-then-synthesize panel built on the existing cognition engine, using concurrent small-model extractors with fixed JSON schemas and a single smart-model judge.
Groups 5, 6, 7, and 8 are marked as joint with the lead or Tim-gated, deferring channel context, multi-session indexing, projection, and fork-fleet launch.
The guide references COMPLETION-CRITERIA.md, RESEARCH-SYNTHESIS.md, and INFERRED-PREFERENCES.md.
It states that build should wait until Tim reacts to the inferred answers in INFERRED-PREFERENCES.md.
It ends with a preservation clause: keep served-embed/rerank paths, structural scan attribution, existing lens APIs, and the non-destructive source guarantee.
standouts: The anti-recency preference lens that treats a cross-session feedback-* corpus as the source of truth for how Tim works.
The explicit rule that small models must only do structured extraction, never judgment or reasoning, with a smart model doing synthesis.
The hard gate on multi-project/multi-session indexing: settle the scheme with the lead before indexing beyond current sessions.
The bridge-free, Company-served, fail-loud recall substrate with freshness checking via source mtime/size stamps.
conventions: Each group follows a principle/sequence/do-don't/files structure.
Uses a tense convention: 'IS' means exists, 'WILL BE' means planned.
References other documents by filename and by [[...]] wiki-style links.
Marks lead-owned or joint work with [P], [JOINT], or explicit 'lead's task' notes.
Flags Tim-gated or far-future work as [TIM-GATED].
Emphasizes additive, non-destructive changes that preserve existing paths and APIs.
concerns: Large portions of the plan (Groups 5-8) are gated on Tim/lead decisions, so the file is partly speculative pending external approval.
Group 4 prescribes specific role/cascade reuse in runtime/cognition.py without confirming the exact APIs exist in this repository.
The feedback-* corpus format and location are referenced but not defined in this file.
A note says session_search.py deprecation must not break other consumers, but no details are given here.
notes: A build plan that maps eight memory-system workstreams to concrete code changes while deferring channel, multi-session, and UI work to Tim/lead approval.
questions: What is the exact format and location of the feedback-* memory corpus referenced by the preferences lens?
What are the precise JSON schemas for each fixed facet extractor and for the final decision brief?
Which role/cascade APIs in runtime/cognition.py exist, and do they support json_schema response_format?
What is the intended registry schema for channel manifests and for project/session addressing?
What does 'uuid-cut probe' mean in practice for mid-conversation resume?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title and introduction state it is a HOW-to-build guide for each group, and the body delivers implementation sequences, file targets, and constraints for each group.
apparent_themes: Additive, non-destructive integration with existing runtime
intention_signals: It is trying to turn a set of design proposals into an actionable, phased implementation roadmap for a channel-memory system that improves recall quality by respecting message structure, avoiding recency bias, and chaining small-model extraction with smart-model synthesis, while staying aligned with the existing Company runtime and channel governance.
novelty: The explicit anti-recency preference lens that treats a cross-session feedback corpus as the source of truth for user preferences, and the structured-extractor panel design that forbids small models from doing judgment or reasoning.
contribution: Provides a concrete, group-by-group implementation plan with file targets, do/don't guardrails, and gating conditions that a fused system can use to sequence memory-system construction without destabilizing existing recall paths.
summary_for_embedding: Implementation guide for building a channel-memory recall stack across eight groups: dimension-aware chunking, served recall with freshness, preference and spin-up lenses, retrieve-then-synthesize panel, channel context, multi-session indexing, projection, and fork-fleet spin-up.
intention_for_embedding: To convert the channel-memory design proposals into an actionable, gated build plan that integrates with the existing Company runtime and cognition engine.
